data.description:
## Why
Chesterton's Fence: "don't remove a fence until you know why it was put up." The plan-review gate has strong ADDITION-side discipline (A1: rule-of-three / YAGNI / NIH / anti-premature-optimization) but NO symmetric REMOVAL-side discipline. An autonomous agent under scope pressure is biased toward "simplifying" by deleting guardrails it does not understand -- exactly the early-trajectory defect this gate exists to catch. This criterion is the removal-side DUAL of A1.

Coverage gap confirmed against current criteria: T4 covers removal's CONSEQUENCES (consumer breakage, reversibility, destructiveness) and E5 partly covers test sync -- but none asks whether the plan UNDERSTANDS why the removed thing existed. You can knowingly tear down a fence with a rollback plan and still not know why it was built, and that passes T4. Net-new gap; NOT in the DSO report (adjacent to but distinct from residual-references / current-vs-future-state).

WS1/WS2 (committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim + E4 exclusion scan) verify a removal-justifying CLAIM ("X is unused") -- necessary but NOT sufficient: a fence's purpose is often invisible to a caller-grep. This criterion demands the deeper grounded demonstration.

## What -- new advisory criterion `removal-rationale`
A code-grounded, LLM-routed, ADVISORY criterion (applies_at leaf; err-toward-surfacing, coach-not-block). Two bright-line triggers (a disjunction -- NO subjective "is this incidental?" judgment). Registered in criteria_routing.json with a new reviewers/plan_review_removal_rationale.md prompt.

### Trigger / GATE (else not-applicable -> PASS). Fires if ANY holds:
1. The plan REMOVES or WEAKENS an externally-observable behavior or contract on ANY path -- including failure / timeout / invalid / boundary / exception semantics. CRITICAL: "internal" is defined by observable-behavior PRESERVATION, not file locality. A refactor that changes how errors/failures/timeouts are handled (swallows an error, changes an exception type, retry->fail-fast, shrinks a timeout) is NOT exempt -- failure-mode behavior is outward-facing.
2. The plan removes/weakens a check / test / validation that GUARDS such a behavior. (Tests IN SCOPE; overlap with E5.changed_behavior_tests is resolved by the Pass-4 coaching pass grouping, not by partitioning the criteria.)
3. The plan removes an artifact carrying an EXPLICIT INTENT MARKER: an explanatory comment, a `# do not remove`, a referenced bug/ticket, or a test named after a bug. (Bright, grep-able -- objective, not a vibe.)

### EXEMPT (PASS / not-applicable): dead-code removal, pure internal simplification that preserves ALL observable behavior incl. error/failure semantics, mechanical/config/doc changes with no behavioral delta. rebar values legitimate simplification -- this criterion must never nag it.

### PASS demonstration (the intent: "show we understand what we're changing," NOT "poke holes"):
The plan must supply a concrete TRIGGERING SCENARIO -- the input/condition under which the removed behavior/guard mattered -- GROUNDED in evidence (the explanatory comment / a pinning test / git-blame / linked ticket / a spec-named input class), NOT invented -- plus evidence the reason no longer applies (handled elsewhere / precondition now guaranteed / contract intentionally changed and updated). The scenario is a specification-by-example of the fence (reuses coach move 6).

### Checklist sub-answers (criterion-local -- NOT generic Pass-2 GRADED_BINARY additions, so genericity is preserved):
- gate: `removes_external_behavior_or_guarded_fence` {yes|no|insufficient} (the disjunction above; no -> not-applicable).
- rationale (only meaningful when gated in): `removal_scenario_grounded` {yes|no|insufficient}: "Does the plan give a concrete scenario where the removed behavior/guard mattered, GROUNDED in a comment/test/blame/linked-ticket (not invented), plus evidence the reason no longer applies?"

### FP guards: two bright-line triggers (no incidental-judgment); grounded-scenario requirement blocks fabricated-scenario false-PASS (thoroughness-theater / self-oracle); explicit exemption preserves refactor/cleanup/dead-code.

### Accepted limitation (LOG in coverage, do not hide -- R-3 / no-silent-caps): a purely-latent guard whose removal changes behavior only for inputs never exercised today AND which carries no intent marker will NOT fire -- it is indistinguishable from dead code without external signal, and chasing it is the un-scalable nag we are avoiding.

## Provenance
Labels like A1 / E5 / T4 / R-3 and "WS-N" are provenance tags from the DSO gap-report evaluation + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020); Chesterton's Fence itself is a net-new gap surfaced in this session (NOT from ~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md). Concrete code targets are in this ticket's file_impact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New advisory criterion `removal-rationale` registered in criteria_routing.json (exec 1-TURN or agentic, facet, applies_at leaf, default_posture advisory) + a reviewers/plan_review_removal_rationale.md prompt. Proof: `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry validate-routing` exits 0.
- [ ] Gate EXEMPTS dead-code / behavior-preserving internal refactor. Proof: recall case in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py where such a plan yields not-applicable; `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k removal_rationale_exempt`.
- [ ] Gate FIRES on an internals change that alters error/failure handling (e.g. swallows an exception / retry->fail-fast). Proof: recall case; `pytest ... -k removal_rationale_error_path`.
- [ ] Gate FIRES on removal of an artifact with an explicit intent marker (commented workaround) even with no happy-path delta. Proof: recall case.
- [ ] Rationale sub-answer requires a GROUNDED scenario: a fabricated/ungrounded justification scores low validity. Proof: recall case with an invented scenario asserting low `removal_scenario_grounded`.
- [ ] Advisory posture confirmed (does not block a claim). Proof: verdict on a triggering ticket is advisory, not block.
- [ ] Coaching reuses move 6 (specification-by-example) to ask for the grounded scenario; E5 test-removal overlap is grouped by the coaching pass (recall case where both fire and coach groups them).
- [ ] The latent-guard limitation is documented in docs/plan-review-gate.md and recorded in the criterion's coverage.
- [ ] docs/plan-review-gate.md documents removal-rationale as the removal-side dual of A1.
data.title: WS11: removal-rationale criterion (Chesterton's Fence, removal-side dual of A1)
